commits.author: usavsolutionsinc
commits.subject: feat(desktop): make auto-update actually work + observable (win now, mac on signing)
commits.body:
The in-app updater never updated because (a) the version never changed and
(b) no Release ever carried the latest.yml/latest-mac.yml metadata electron-updater
requires. This wires up a real, verifiable auto-update feed.

- CI: publish a GitHub Release v<version> on each version bump, uploading the
  installers AND the update metadata (latest.yml / latest-mac.yml / blockmaps).
  Windows publishes even if the macOS job fails (e.g. signing secrets not yet set).
- Windows: works now — public repo, NSIS updates unsigned. Space-free artifact
  names so GitHub's space->dot asset rename can't 404 the update URL.
- macOS: add Developer ID hardened-runtime signing + notarization + a zip target
  (Squirrel.Mac updates from a zip, not a dmg) + latest-mac.yml. Activates once
  the Apple secrets (CSC_LINK, CSC_KEY_PASSWORD, APPLE_ID,
  APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD, APPLE_TEAM_ID) are added to the repo.
- Observability: route electron-updater through electron-log so the update
  lifecycle (checking/available/downloaded/error) is written to main.log.

commits.author: usavsolutionsinc
commits.subject: feat(receiving): derive delivery from physical dock scan when carrier lags
commits.body:
Carrier "delivered" is absent or late for most inbound boxes: of 1,291 scanned
cartons, 728 were scanned (707 fully opened) while the carrier still said NOT
delivered — USPS access-blocked/NULL-status (540), unknown-carrier numbers that
never poll (165), FedEx stale (22); when it does land the lag runs to ~87 days
for USPS. The physical scan is the real delivery truth.

Add a 'receiving_scan' pass to runReconcileDeliveredJob: for an inbound shipment
matched by the same last-8 / shipment-link rule the Incoming display uses, set
is_delivered + is_terminal, stamp the earliest matching scan as delivered_at,
and stop polling. Gated by INBOUND_SHIPMENT_PREDICATE so an outbound order
shipment whose tracking last-8 coincidentally collides with a scan is never
flipped (warranty-safe). Extract SHIPMENT_SCAN_MATCH_CONDITION so the EXISTS
predicate and the delivered_at derivation share one match definition.

Scanned boxes are excluded from delivered-unscanned by construction, so flipping
is_delivered cannot resurface them (verified: 0 receiving_scan rows in the
delivered-unscanned base). Backfilled 766 inbound shipments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.title: derive delivery from physical dock scan when carrier lags

commits.author: usavsolutionsinc
commits.subject: fix(receiving): drop scanned boxes from Incoming via last-8 tracking match
commits.body:
Incoming only dropped a row when the Zoho-PO line advanced off EXPECTED, but a
dock scan/unbox writes to the carton's receiving_scans and never touches that
line (receiving_id is usually NULL), so scanned boxes stayed stuck in Incoming
and rendered as delivery_state='UNKNOWN'.

Read-side: add one SHIPMENT_SCANNED_PREDICATE — the last 8 of the Zoho-pasted
tracking number matched inside the scanned barcode digits (Zoho-pasted and
scanned values are different representations of the same package), plus a
shipment-link fallback. Apply it across the Incoming base, the DELIVERED_UNOPENED
facet/CASE, the summary chip counts + per-carrier CTE, and the canonical
delivered-unscanned tile, so a scanned box leaves Incoming the instant a scan
lands and count === rows.

Write-path twin: extend runTrackingMatchReconcileJob (reconcile-delivered cron)
with a D4 pass that advances a still-EXPECTED line to MATCHED + attaches it to
the scanned carton on the same last-8 match, so the line truly progresses in the
data model rather than only being hidden by the read-side guard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.title: drop scanned boxes from Incoming via last-8 tracking match

the Incoming queue. Only the first was on a cron (incoming-po-sync); the
mirror step was never scheduled, so the clearing only happened on a manual
button press. Schedule the existing /api/cron/zoho/po-sync route:
  - mode=delta every 15 min (offset :07/:22/:37/:52 to not collide with
    incoming-po-sync at :00/:15/:30/:45)
  - mode=full nightly as a drift safety net

Together with incoming-po-sync this reproduces the button's behavior on a
cron. Requires a production deploy to register.

commits.body:
Deep-scanned each flow's actual status vocabulary and rebuilt the descriptive
flows panel to match how the real pages display them — not generic lifecycle
states. Each step now carries the display label, the real status key, the
station, the activity/event signal, and the route that performs the transition.

FBA (fba_shipment_status_enum · src/lib/fba/status.ts):
  Planned(PLANNED, FNSKU_SCANNED, /api/tech/scan)
  → Tested("ready to go", /api/fba/items/ready)
  → Packed(/api/fba/items/scan)
  → Combined(LABEL_ASSIGNED — UI label is "Combined" — /api/fba/labels/bind)
  → Shipped(/api/fba/shipments/mark-shipped); +Out of Stock / Closed

Receiving (inbound_workflow_status_enum · workflow-stages.ts):
  Incoming → Scanned → Matched → Unboxed → Awaiting Test → Testing → Passed
  → Done; +Failed / RTV / Scrap

Outbound (order_unit_allocations.state):
  Allocated → Picked → Packed → Labeled → Shipped; +Released, carrier
  Accepted→In Transit→Delivered, and the Awaiting/Pending/Shipped UI buckets

Repair (repair_service.status, free-text): Incoming Shipment → Pending Repair
  → Awaiting Parts → Repaired, Contact Customer → Awaiting Pickup/Payment
  → Picked Up/Shipped; +Cancelled

Returns/RMA (rma_authorizations.status): Authorized → Received → Dispositioned
  (ACCEPT/HOLD/RTV/REWORK/SCRAP) → Closed; +Expired/Canceled, orders_exceptions

Display adds: per-flow source file, numbered step rail, real status-key chips,
signal + route annotations, and a "branches & terminal states" footer. Still
read-only (interactivity deferred). tsc clean; build ✓.

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: workflow: engine core — contract, registry, router, runtime, advance (Phase B)
commits.body:
Pure-TypeScript node engine, no existing code touched. All collaborators are
injected so routing/advance are unit-testable without DB or Ably:

  contract.ts  NodeDefinition/NodeContext/NodeResult + WorkflowStore boundary
  registry.ts  node-type registry (register/get/list, palette meta)
  router.ts    selectNextTarget — pure conditional routing over edges
  runtime.ts   runNode — times a node, maps thrown errors to the `error` port
  advance.ts   advanceItem — lock → run → record → emit → route/park/done
  store.ts     Drizzle WorkflowStore; passes organizationId EXPLICITLY because
               the neon-http client can't see the app.current_org GUC
  events.ts    emitWorkflowEvent → existing publishDbEvent (no-ops w/o Ably)
  index.ts     production wiring + registerBuiltins (nodes land in Phase C)

The concurrency lock is an injectable AdvanceLock (NULL_LOCK default); a real
Upstash lock gets wired when the live advance route lands in Phase D.

Tests: 13 passing (router + advance: linear, fail→repair branch, terminal,
await-park, thrown error, unknown type, context merge, not-enrolled, terminal
no-op, held-lock no double-advance). Added `npm run test:workflow`. tsc clean.

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: test(e2e): parts auto-sort spec (grade→PARTS sorts to TECH-PARTS bin)
commits.body:
Authenticated Playwright API spec covering the For-Parts auto-sort:
  1. seed a throwaway serial, grade PARTS → unit STOCKED in TECH-PARTS bin
     (parts_sorted:true), then delete the seed (zero residue)
  2. committed unit (ALLOCATED/SHIPPED) graded PARTS → parts_sorted:false,
     location unchanged
  3. PATCH a MATCHED receiving line to PARTS → needs_test cleared
Each test self-skips when data is absent and restores state in finally so it's
idempotent against the live DB.

commits.subject: feat(receiving): auto-sort "For Parts" units to the Technical Room parts bin
commits.body:

flow and is auto-sorted into a pickable parts bin (STOCKED, so it stays sellable
and usable as repair stock). Fires from the grade endpoint (inventory unit panel

(clears needs_test so the line drops out of the test queue, then sorts its
serials). Guards skip units already committed to an order/shipped or already in
the bin; failures never fail the grade/PATCH write.

- receiving-lines PATCH: condition=PARTS -> needs_test=false + sort serials

commits.subject: fix(receiving): label tracks selected unit condition on multi-qty lines
commits.body:
A PO line with >1 of the same item splits into per-unit rows
(ReceivingUnitRows), each with its own condition grade. But the label
preview/print used the line-level `cond`, so it always showed the line
default (rendered as "New" when empty) regardless of which unit was

(saved → pending → line default) via onActiveConditionChange, re-arming
the emit on line switch. LineEditPanel feeds that into the label payload's
conditionCode for multi-qty lines, so selecting an item refreshes the
label's condition; single-qty lines are unchanged.

commits.author: usavsolutionsinc
commits.subject: fix(auth): assign staff_roles on create/invite + honor persistent sessions
commits.body:
Two production auth bugs:

1. Packer/role FORBIDDEN: effective permissions resolve only from
   staff_roles×roles, but POST /api/admin/staff and /staff/invite wrote
   staff.role without inserting a staff_roles row — created staff resolved
   to an empty permission set and 403'd on every gated route while the UI
   still showed their role. Both endpoints now insert the matching
   staff_roles assignment (transactional) + invalidate the role cache;
   invite also canonicalizes/validates the role key.

2. Persistent login not sticking: signin bound sessions to shift end and
   createSession took min(shiftEnd, policyWindow), collapsing the
   persistent 1-year window to midnight; /api/auth/session slid the DB
   expiry but never re-issued the cookie, so the browser dropped it on
   lid-close/next day. persistent staff now bypass shift-bound expiry, and
   the heartbeat re-issues usav_sid with the slid max-age each call.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.title: assign staff_roles on create/invite + honor persistent sessions

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: feat(inventory): tabbed sidebar overhaul + activity inbox in FAB popover
commits.body:
## Inventory sidebar

Replace the single flat inventory sidebar with a 6-tab structure modeled
after the dashboard/shipped sidebar pattern:

- Activity (default landing) · Bins · SKUs · Units · Alerts · Counts
- Per-tab scoped-search field pills (All / Bin Barcode / Zone / Room /
  SKU Held; All / SKU / Product Title / Brand; etc.)
- Multi-select bucket filter chips with server-side counts where the
  endpoint exposes them
- 250ms debounced TanStack search with per-tab cache keys
- Per-tab recent searches in localStorage
- Cross-tab handoff card surfaces when another tab has more matches for
  the active query
- Deep-linkable URL state: `/inventory/<tab>?q=…&field=…&filter=…&open=…`
- Sub-route pages under /inventory/{activity,bins,skus,units,alerts,counts}

## Detail pane

Row clicks now fill the main right pane (not a slide-in overlay).
Selection is driven by `?open=<kind>:<publicRef>` so refresh restores
state. ESC and `[` / `]` keys close / walk prev-next through the
current result list.

Detail panels:
- BinDetailsPanel / SkuDetailsPanel / UnitDetailsPanel reuse existing
  ByBin / BySku / ByUnit views
- UnitDetailsPanel adds grade + hold/release action cards on top of the
  unit timeline
- AlertDetailsPanel — read-only feed view + Acknowledge button
- CountCampaignDetailsPanel — campaign progress + status

## API

New read endpoints:
- GET /api/inventory/alerts — stock_alerts with bucket counts
- GET /api/inventory/counts — cycle_count_campaigns with derived status
  + per-line progress

New mutation endpoints:
- POST /api/inventory/alerts/[id]/ack — sets resolved_at, idempotent
- POST /api/serial-units/[id]/grade — updates condition_grade, appends
  inventory_event + serial_unit_condition_history (linked via event id)

Reuses existing /api/serial-units/[id]/hold + /release for hold actions.

Adds permissions: `stock_alerts.ack`, `serial_units.grade`.

## Activity inbox

Relocate the bottom-left ActivityInboxDock into the Quick Access FAB
popover as an "Actions" row with a Bell icon and a rose-colored unread
badge. Click opens a secondary ActivityInboxPopover (same pattern as
Phone history). ActivityInboxDock export retained for opt-in callers
but no longer auto-mounted from ResponsiveLayout.

commits.author: usavsolutionsinc
commits.subject: feat(electron): Edit menu + draggable top strip
commits.body:
Two long-missing shell affordances:

1. Edit menu (cut/copy/paste/select-all). The menu was just View + Window,
   so Cmd+V was never routed through Electron's role-based clipboard
   handler — meaning paste into embedded <webview> content (the Zoho login
   form via the new ZohoSplitPane) silently failed. Adding the Edit
   submenu — plus the standard macOS app submenu — wires Cmd+C / Cmd+V /
   Cmd+X / Cmd+A into the focused guest page.

2. ElectronDragStrip. titleBarStyle:'hiddenInset' removes the native title
   bar but the renderer's content covered y=0 with no app-region:drag,
   leaving the window un-draggable. The new component is a fixed 28px
   strip at the top: WebkitAppRegion:'drag' with pointer-events:none, so
   mousedown drags the window while regular clicks pass through to the
   UI below. On macOS we leave a 78px gap for the traffic-light buttons.
   Mounted in app/layout.tsx; only renders when desktopApp.isElectron.
commits.title: Edit menu + draggable top strip

commits.author: usavsolutionsinc
commits.subject: feat(receiving): optimistic receive, idempotent rescans, Zoho slide-in pane
commits.body:
OVER_RECEIVE → graceful: receiveLineUnits short-circuits when the same serial is already on this line, returning already_received instead of throwing. UI swaps the over-receive confirm dialog for a success toast in that case. Idempotency wrapper: scan-serial + mark-received-po now cache responses in api_idempotency_responses by (Idempotency-Key | client_event_id, route), so network retries replay the same answer instead of re-running. Optimistic Receive: mark-received-po returns success as soon as local DB writes commit. Lines eager-promote to DONE locally (SoT is here, not Zoho). createPurchaseReceive + description PUT + DONE-gate moved into after(); Zoho "already created a receive for all the items" is treated as success. Operator sees 1/1 + green dot immediately; Zoho catches up in the background. Connection-pool fix: threaded the receiveLineUnits transaction client into the ledger / tech_serial_numbers / inventory_events writes that previously used pool.query — they were grabbing a second connection out of poolMax=3 in prod and tripping the 30s query_timeout under concurrent receivers. Dot color sync: every receiving status dot (mobile rows + carton sheet + station table + recent rail) now turns emerald when received >= expected, matching the green qty text instead of staying blue on MATCHED. Zoho slide-in: per-line flow header gets an "Open in Zoho" action that reveals an in-app right-side pane (ZohoSplitPane). Electron mounts the embedded webview; browser tabs see an "Open in Zoho" link. Draggable left edge resizes; width is persisted to localStorage. No popup windows. Hydration fix: PoLinesAccordion was nesting a
chip inside a

.

commits.subject: plan: Products / Inventory / Warehouse rename + modernization
commits.body:
Comprehensive UX/UI plan for renaming two existing top-level routes
and introducing a first-class Products page. Saved to
context/page-rename-products-inventory-warehouse-plan.md.

The rename map:
  /sku-stock  →  /inventory   (live ledger — search by SKU / unit /
                                location / state / condition)
  /inventory  →  /warehouse   (the page that's currently misnamed —
                                rooms, bins, fill bars, physical map)
  /products   →  NEW          (sku_catalog editor — search, edit,
                                bulk sync, click-into-inventory)

Why: today's labels don't match what each page actually shows. The
existing /inventory route renders WarehouseMap + RoomsBoard + BinsTable
— it's the warehouse, not the ledger. Renaming makes the navigation
match the operator's mental model:
  Products    "what do we sell?"
  Inventory   "where is each unit right now?"
  Warehouse   "what's in this physical location?"

Plan covers (15 sections, ~800 lines):
  - naming rationale + back-compat redirects via next.config.js
  - file + component + sidebar rename map (with the conflict-order
    trick: warehouse rename FIRST so the inventory slot frees up)
  - information architecture (one job per page, with what each page
    does NOT do)
  - cross-page navigation graph
  - per-page UX/UI:
      * Products: virtualized catalog table + filter chips + detail
        page with attributes/platform-links/kit/QC/live-stock summary
      * Inventory: universal search + filter chips + four views
        (pulse / by-sku / by-bin / by-unit) on one URL surface
      * Warehouse: three view modes (map / list / print labels) with
        DRIFT-alert banner and open-cycle-count strip
  - shared primitives (StatusBadge, ConditionBadge, ScanInput, etc.)
  - mobile/narrow-viewport behavior
  - consistent search+filter grammar (one ScanInput across all three)
  - realtime Ably channels + cache tag invalidation contract
  - permission gating (reuses sku_stock.* + bin.* + print.label)
  - 4-phase rollout (A: routes exist alongside; B: modernize behind
    feature flags; C: flip flags + relabel sidebar; D: delete legacy)
  - file-level implementation plan: 15 commits, ~2.5-3.5 weeks
  - 8 open decisions captured for follow-up

No code changes — plan-only commit. Implementation follows once the
plan is approved.

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: inventory v2 #4: Zoho order-intake webhook + auto-allocation
commits.body:
Operationalizes Phase 4 end-to-end. Zoho POSTs an order-created
webhook, the receiver normalizes the payload, the shared ingest
helper upserts orders rows + auto-allocates (when the flag is on).

src/lib/inventory/order-intake.ts (NEW):
  - ingestOrder({externalId, source, lineItems, ...}):
      1. Per line: SELECT existing orders row by (order_id, sku) →
         INSERT if missing (status='open', account_source = source).
         Transactional batch — partial failures roll back.
      2. Per resulting orders row: call allocateOrder() with a
         deterministic clientEventId derived from
         "order-intake:{source}:{externalId}:{sku}" so retries idempo-
         tent. Allocation runs in its own transaction so one failing
         line doesn't roll back its siblings.
      3. Returns {externalId, source, totalLines, unitsAllocated,
         lines[]}. Each line carries {sku, orderId, created,
         allocation: AllocateOrderResult | null}. null when the flag
         is off (helper still creates orders rows, skips alloc).
  - Source string validated against /[a-z0-9_-]+/i so future eBay /
    manual integrations get the same path.

src/app/api/webhooks/zoho/orders/route.ts (NEW):
  - POST handler:
      * Fails CLOSED when ZOHO_WEBHOOK_SECRET env var is unset — 503.
      * Returns 401 on header mismatch
        (x-zoho-webhook-secret: $ZOHO_WEBHOOK_SECRET).
      * extractZohoOrder() pulls salesorder_id + line_items out of
        either {"salesorder": {...}} or top-level shapes (Zoho
        Inventory vs Books vs subtype variation). Tolerates missing
        fields; returns null when payload doesn't look like an order
        (ping events, malformed bodies).
      * Lines with no SKU are dropped silently.
  - GET probe: returns {ok, webhook, configured} so Zoho's "Test"
    button reports the secret-presence status without exposing it.

Idempotency:
  - orders rows: dedup on (order_id, sku) — re-running the same
    webhook doesn't double-create.
  - allocations: per-line clientEventId on inventory_events ON
    CONFLICT DO NOTHING.
  - All allocation failures are logged but never break the response
    so Zoho doesn't see retries for downstream issues.

Operations:
  1. Set ZOHO_WEBHOOK_SECRET in Vercel env to a long random string.
  2. Configure Zoho Inventory webhook for "Sales order created" →
     POST https://app.example/api/webhooks/zoho/orders with header
     x-zoho-webhook-secret: <that secret>.
  3. Flip INVENTORY_V2_ALLOCATION on Vercel when ready.

Zero new tsc errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.title: inventory v2 #4: Zoho order-intake webhook + auto-allocation

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: inventory v2 #2: cycle count admin UI
commits.body:
Operationalizes the Phase 0 cycle_count_campaigns + cycle_count_lines
tables (2026-05-14 migration). No UI existed; this lands the full
operator + admin workflow.

src/lib/inventory/cycle-count.ts (NEW):
  - createCampaign(name, varianceTol, createdByStaffId) — INSERTs
    the campaign + snapshots every bin_contents row (qty > 0 OR
    last_counted IS NULL) into cycle_count_lines (status='pending').
    Returns {campaignId, lineCount}.
  - submitCount(lineId, countedQty) — auto-routes to status='counted'
    when within campaign.variance_tol, status='pending_review' when
    outside. Computes variance + variance fraction inline.
  - approveLine(lineId) — for variance != 0, INSERTs sku_stock_ledger
    with reason='CYCLE_COUNT_ADJ'; updates bin_contents.qty +
    last_counted to match counted_qty; flips line to 'approved'.
  - rejectLine(lineId) — line → 'rejected'; no stock changes.
  - closeCampaign(campaignId) — auto-approves remaining 'counted'
    lines (inline to keep one transaction, since pg disallows nested
    transactions); leaves 'pending_review' alone; flips campaign
    status to 'closed' + stamps closed_at.

src/app/admin/inventory-v2/cycle-counts/page.tsx (NEW):
  - Create-campaign form (name + variance_tol).
  - Last-50 campaigns table with per-status counts (total / counted
    / pending_review / approved). Pending-review counter goes amber

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: inventory v2: drift-check cron + DRIFT stock-alert type
commits.body:
Catches the moment sku_stock.stock (or .boxed_stock) disagrees with
SUM(sku_stock_ledger) per dimension — the canary that a writer
bypassed the ledger trigger and quantities went out of sync.

src/lib/migrations/2026-05-18_stock_alerts_drift.sql:
  - Extends stock_alerts_type_chk to permit a fourth alert_type:
    'DRIFT'. Pure CHECK rewrite, idempotent. Applied vs Neon
    (neondb) at deploy time — constraint now reads
    CHECK (alert_type IN ('LOW_STOCK','NEVER_COUNTED','STALE_COUNT','DRIFT')).

src/app/api/qstash/inventory/drift-check/route.ts:
  POST handler (isQStashOrigin gated):
    1. INSERT stock_alerts rows of alert_type='DRIFT' for every SKU
       in v_sku_stock_drift. ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING dedupes against
       the idx_stock_alerts_open partial unique idx so one open
       alert per SKU. qty_at_trigger = max(|warehouse Δ|, |boxed Δ|).
    2. Resolve any open DRIFT alerts whose SKU is no longer drifted
       (the trigger projection caught up).
    3. Return { opened, resolved, worst[5], elapsed_ms } so QStash
       logs are useful at a glance.
  GET handler: tiny health probe { ok: true, queue: 'qstash',
  job: 'inventory-drift-check' }.

src/config/qstash-schedules.json:
  Adds inventory-drift-check-daily-1100-utc at "0 11 * * *" (between
  the existing refresh-reports 10:30 UTC and stock-alerts 14:00 UTC).
  Run /api/qstash/schedules/bootstrap (or scripts/bootstrap-qstash-
  schedules.mjs) after this deploys to register the schedule.

Designed as a passive safety net — drift should always be empty when
the trigger is healthy. If it's ever non-empty, the alert surfaces
in /admin/inventory-v2's drift section AND in stock_alerts so the
existing alerting pipeline catches it.

commits.subject: QR label migration: QR-only print template + GTIN backfill ran
commits.body:
Two more pieces of the QR-only / title-left / QR-right unit-label
migration. Lands the print-template surgery and the one-time GTIN
backfill for every existing sku_catalog row.

src/lib/print/printProductLabel.ts — QR-only template:
  - Drops the JsBarcode CDN script + 1D barcode-row entirely. The
    1D barcode under the SKU code is gone.
  - Layout: title + serial + sku stacked on the LEFT, QR on the
    RIGHT (0.88in × 0.88in, was 0.86in to give the SVG a touch more
    bleed). Title font promoted (12px bold), serial promoted (14px
    monospace bold) since it's now the primary visual identifier.
  - QR is pre-rendered as inline SVG via react-qr-code (already a
    dep). No remote script wait; popup waitMs dropped from 600 → 200.
  - PrintProductLabelInput extended with optional { qrPayload, gtin }.
    Resolution order: qrPayload (e.g. from /api/units/next-id) →
    GS1 Digital Link via buildGs1UnitUrl(QR_BASE_URL, gtin, serial)
    → legacy /m/u/{serial} → /sku-stock/{sku}. Callers that don't
    know the GTIN still work — the GS1 form just doesn't apply.
  - PrintProductLabelsInput extended with { gtin, qrPayloads[] }
    so the multi-print path passes the shared GTIN once and per-
    label QR payloads index-aligned with serialNumbers.

scripts/backfill-internal-gtins.mjs — one-time stamper:
  - For every sku_catalog row where gtin IS NULL OR '': generate the
    deterministic GTIN-14 ('02' + 11-digit zero-padded id + check
    digit) and persist via UPDATE … SET gtin = COALESCE(NULLIF(gtin,
    ''), $candidate). Concurrent /api/units/next-id calls take their
    own COALESCE path; the first writer wins, conflicts are counted
    as a non-error.
  - --dry-run and --limit N flags. Idempotent; re-running processes
    only remaining unstamped rows.

Backfill ran against Neon (neondb): 1194 sku_catalog rows stamped,
0 conflicts, 0 failures, 0 remaining without GTIN. Every existing
SKU is now QR-printable without an extra round-trip.

Still pending (was paused due to active edits): MultiSkuSnBarcode.tsx +
BarcodePreview.tsx rewrite to call /api/units/next-id and render QR
in the in-app preview. Will land after the parallel edits there settle.

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: QR label migration: internal-GTIN + unit-ID + /api/units/next-id (infra)
commits.body:
Lands the supporting infrastructure for the upcoming QR-only / title-
left / QR-right unit-label format. Zero existing-flow change; the UI
surgery on MultiSkuSnBarcode + BarcodePreview + printProductLabel
follows in a separate commit so it doesn't collide with parallel work
on those files.

Decisions baked in (2026-05-18):
  - QR-only labels, no 1D barcode in the unit-label preview / print path.
  - Title-left / QR-right preview layout.
  - Internal pseudo-GTIN-14 per SKU (prefix `02` reserved-internal range).
  - New unit-id format {SKU_SHORT}-{YEAR}-{SEQ6} replaces legacy `:A01`.
  - Condition is database-only; QR encodes only the unit identity.
  - Internal-only QR scanning; production app URL is the QR origin.

New files:
  src/lib/inventory/internal-gtin.ts
    - gs1CheckDigit(13-digit body) — mod-10 algorithm with alternating
      3/1 weights right-to-left. Validated against known GS1 demo
      00614141999996 (computes check digit 6).
    - generateInternalGtin(skuCatalogId) — deterministic 14-digit:
      "02" + 11-digit zero-padded id + check digit.
    - getOrCreateInternalGtin — read sku_catalog.gtin; if empty, mint
      one and persist via UPDATE … SET gtin = COALESCE(NULLIF(gtin,''),
      $candidate). Safe under concurrency (first writer wins).
    - isValidGtin14 sanity helper.

  src/lib/inventory/unit-id.ts
    - shortSku(text) — uppercase + strip non-[A-Z0-9-] + 20-char cap.
    - formatUnitId(short, year, seq) — `{short}-{year}-{seq6}`.
    - allocateNextUnitId(skuCatalogId, skuText, yearOverride?) — uses
      Phase 0's fn_next_unit_seq for atomic per-(sku, year) allocation.

  src/app/api/units/next-id/route.ts
    - POST endpoint. Body: { sku } or { sku_catalog_id }.
    - Resolves sku_catalog row → ensures GTIN → allocates unit-id →
      builds buildGs1UnitUrl(origin, gtin, unitId). Returns
      { unitId, gtin, qrUrl, skuCatalogId, sku, productTitle, year, seq, skuShort }.
    - Single round trip from the printer UI; replaces the legacy
      /api/sku-manager?action=current then ?action=increment dance.
    - Permission gate: print.label.

Modified:
  src/app/api/get-title-by-sku/route.ts
    - Response now includes skuCatalogId + gtin (additive — existing
      callers reading title/stock/location/imageUrl are unaffected).

  src/utils/barcode.ts
    - Adds loadQrLibrary / renderQr / generateQrDataUrl using the
      `qrcode` npm package. Dynamic import so the ~30KB doesn't land
      in pages that don't print labels.
    - Existing JsBarcode loaders + renderers KEPT — receiving labels,
      walk-in receipts, and ProductLabelPreview still use 1D barcodes;
      only the unit-label preview drops to QR-only.

Dependencies:
  package.json — adds qrcode + @types/qrcode.

Zero new tsc errors in any of these files.

- Reads QSTASH_TOKEN + NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL from .env.

Bootstrap was run against production QStash: 10 schedules now
registered, including the two cron-migration entries from this
session (stock-alerts, refresh-reports) and an orphaned
shipping-sync-all-carriers-5pm-weekdays-pacific that was in the
config but had never been bootstrapped.

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: Move Vercel crons to QStash (zero Vercel cron billing)
commits.body:
Removes both jobs from vercel.json so Vercel stops invoicing for cron
invocations. The same two jobs continue to run on the same daily UTC
schedule via QStash, which we already pay for.

vercel.json:
- crons array emptied. The file stays in place; future Vercel-specific
  settings (rewrites, headers) can land here without re-creating it.

src/config/qstash-schedules.json — two new entries:
- stock-alerts-daily-1400-utc      cron "0 14 * * *"  → /api/cron/stock-alerts
- refresh-reports-daily-1030-utc   cron "30 10 * * *" → /api/cron/refresh-reports
  (same UTC times the Vercel crons used, so no behavior change.)

src/app/api/cron/stock-alerts/route.ts &
src/app/api/cron/refresh-reports/route.ts:
- Convert the cron logic from GET to POST so QStash can drive it
  (QStash publishJSON always POSTs). Auth-gate the POST with
  isQStashOrigin (signature or Bearer $QSTASH_TOKEN).
- Add a trivial GET health probe — no auth, no work, just returns
  { ok: true, queue: 'qstash', job: ... }. Matches the pattern used
  by every other route under /api/qstash/.
- Add `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` and `maxDuration` so
  the routes don't get statically rendered and have enough runtime
  budget for the materialized-view refresh.

Activation:
After this deploys, POST /api/qstash/schedules/bootstrap to register
the two new schedules in QStash. The bootstrap route deletes obsolete
schedules automatically, so the previous Vercel cron entries (now
gone) have no equivalent to remove on the QStash side.

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: inventory v2 Phase 7: returns intake + hold/release (flagged)
commits.body:
Per context/inventory_system_upgrade_plan.md §6.7, §6.8 and §10 Phase 7.
Three new endpoints behind INVENTORY_V2_RETURNS (OFF by default).

POST /api/returns/intake:
  - For each scanned/identified unit (serials raw or GS1 URLs, or
    explicit serial_unit_ids):
      * sku_stock_ledger row +1 reason='RETURN_CUSTOMER' (trigger
        projects the qty back onto sku_stock.stock automatically).
      * serial_units.current_status → RETURNED.
      * inventory_events RETURNED with prev_status reflecting the actual
        prior lifecycle state, stock_ledger_id linked.
  - All in one transaction; rejects with the missing serials/ids if any
    input cannot be resolved, leaving zero mutations committed.
  - Body: { tracking_number?, order_id?, reason?, serials?[],
            serial_unit_ids?[], client_event_id? }.
  - Permission: receiving.mark_received (returns physically arrive at
    the receiving dock).

POST /api/serial-units/[id]/hold:
  - Transitions any non-ON_HOLD unit to ON_HOLD.
  - Stashes the previous lifecycle state in the HELD inventory_event's
    payload.restore_status so the release endpoint can roll it back.
  - Requires a `reason` body field.
  - Returns 409 if the unit is already ON_HOLD (no double-hold).
  - Permission: sku_stock.adjust.

POST /api/serial-units/[id]/release:
  - Companion to hold. Reads the most recent HELD event's payload.
    restore_status to recover the pre-hold state; falls back to
    STOCKED if absent/invalid.
  - `force_status` body field overrides the auto-recovery — e.g. send
    'TRIAGED' to push a held unit back into the refurb flow instead of
    straight to STOCKED.
  - Returns 409 if the unit isn't currently ON_HOLD.
  - Emits RELEASED_HOLD event with prev='ON_HOLD', next=restored.
  - Permission: sku_stock.adjust.

All single-transaction. Zero new tsc errors.

This is the last functional phase. Phase 8 in the plan is "Reporting,
cycle counts, polish (ongoing)" — UI work + dashboards that build on
the inventory_events / sku_stock_ledger primitives all six prior
phases just wired up.

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: inventory v2 Phase 6: FBA serial linkage + per-unit decrement (flagged)
commits.body:
Per context/inventory_system_upgrade_plan.md §6.6 and §10 Phase 6.
Two new endpoints behind INVENTORY_V2_FBA_SERIAL_LINK (OFF by default).

POST /api/fba/items/[id]/link-unit:
  - Resolves a scanned serial (raw or GS1 Digital Link URL) or
    explicit serial_unit_id.
  - INSERT fba_shipment_item_units (composite PK provides idempotency
    on retry) tying the unit to the FBA shipment item line.
  - If the unit is currently STOCKED, transitions it → ALLOCATED and
    emits an ALLOCATED inventory_events row (station='PACK',
    payload.source='fba.link-unit'). Already-ALLOCATED units (e.g.
    re-scanned after a brief pause) stay put.
  - Body: { scan? | serial_unit_id?, client_event_id? }.

POST /api/fba/shipments/[id]/ship-units:
  - For every linked serial_unit on the shipment (joined through
    fba_shipment_items → fba_shipment_item_units) NOT already SHIPPED:
      * inventory_events PACKED (prev=current, next='PACKED')
      * inventory_events LABELED (prev='PACKED', next='LABELED')
      * sku_stock_ledger row delta=-1 reason='SOLD' dimension='WAREHOUSE'
      * serial_units.current_status → SHIPPED
      * inventory_events SHIPPED with stock_ledger_id linked
  - All in one transaction; FOR UPDATE OF su locks each unit row.
  - Idempotent — re-running after success finds zero non-SHIPPED units
    and returns { shipped_unit_count: 0 }.
  - Body: { client_event_id? }.

The existing /api/fba/shipments/close endpoint is untouched. It
continues to drive the FBA shipment status machine for non-serialized
lines. Phase 6's two new endpoints add the per-unit decrement that
the legacy close never performed — Tier-3 FBA shipments now produce
the same sku_stock_ledger trail as direct orders.

Permission gate: fba.stage_shipments (existing). Zero new tsc errors.

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: inventory v2 Phase 5: pack/ship single-decrement transaction (flagged)
commits.body:
Per context/inventory_system_upgrade_plan.md §6.5 and §10 Phase 5.
This is THE CORE FIX. Behind INVENTORY_V2_PACKING (OFF by default).

POST /api/pack/ship is a single transaction that:

  1. Resolves every input serial (raw text or GS1 Digital Link URL via
     Phase 1's parseScannedUrl) and serial_unit_id explicitly, then
     FOR UPDATEs the matching serial_units rows.
  2. Validates that EVERY unit has an open (non-RELEASED) allocation
     for THIS order in a pre-SHIPPED state. On any mismatch the
     transaction returns 409 with the offending units and ZERO writes
     are committed.
  3. Per unit, in order:
       - order_unit_allocations.state → SHIPPED
       - inventory_events PACKED  (idempotent via clientEventId:unit:PACKED)
       - inventory_events LABELED (idempotent via :LABELED suffix)
       - sku_stock_ledger row delta=-1 reason='SOLD' dimension='WAREHOUSE'
         (the trg_sku_stock_from_ledger trigger projects this onto
          sku_stock.stock atomically — THE DECREMENT)
       - serial_units.current_status → SHIPPED
       - inventory_events SHIPPED (idempotent, stock_ledger_id linked)
  4. Writes ONE packer_logs row (ORDERS type) keyed to the shipment so
     the existing shipped-dashboard query keeps working unchanged.
  5. Writes ONE station_activity_logs row (PACK / PACK_SHIPPED) for
     cross-station visibility.
  6. Flips orders.status='shipped' LAST so any concurrent reader sees
     the event-side state before the order flag changes.

Off-flag: returns 503 immediately so callers fall back to the legacy
/api/packing-logs path. No production behavior changes until the flag
flips and operators are running the v2 receive → triage → test →
allocate → pick → ship flow end-to-end.

Body:
  {
    order_id: number,
    serials?: string[],
    serial_unit_ids?: number[],
    tracking_number?: string,
    carrier?: string,
    client_event_id?: string
  }

Permission gate: packing.complete_order (existing packer permission).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.title: inventory v2 Phase 5: pack/ship single-decrement transaction (flagged)

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: inventory v2 Phase 4: order allocation + pick scan (flagged)
commits.body:
Per context/inventory_system_upgrade_plan.md §6.3, §6.4 and §10 Phase 4.
Three new endpoints behind INVENTORY_V2_ALLOCATION (OFF by default).

This is the phase that fixes the double-counting problem described in
the plan: from here on, an order does not consume "available" stock at
handoff — it reserves a specific unit via order_unit_allocations, and
the unit stays in the same physical state machine while the order
walks ALLOCATED → PICKED → PACKED → SHIPPED. The single decrement
event lands in Phase 5.

POST /api/orders/[id]/allocate:
  - Loads orders.{sku, quantity, condition}.
  - SELECT serial_units WHERE current_status='STOCKED' AND sku=$
      [AND condition_grade=$], ORDER BY id ASC, LIMIT $qty,
      FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED (concurrent allocators get disjoint sets).
  - INSERT order_unit_allocations row per unit (state='ALLOCATED').
    idx_oua_open_unit (partial UNIQUE on serial_unit_id WHERE state
    != 'RELEASED') from Phase 0 guarantees no double-allocation.
  - UPDATE serial_units SET current_status='ALLOCATED'.
  - INSERT inventory_events ALLOCATED (prev='STOCKED', next='ALLOCATED',
    payload.allocation_id, payload.ordinal).
  - Returns partial=true with the actual count when fewer units are
    available than requested.
  - Body: { quantity?, condition_grade?, client_event_id? }.

POST /api/orders/[id]/release:
  - Snapshots every non-RELEASED order_unit_allocations row for the order
    via FOR UPDATE.
  - UPDATE state='RELEASED', released_at, released_reason per row.
  - Returns unit to STOCKED only if current_status is an outbound state
    (ALLOCATED/PICKED/PACKED/LABELED/STAGED) — defensive against
    SHIPPED units that shouldn't have an open allocation.
  - Emits RELEASED events with prev_status reflecting the unit's pre-release
    state. Idempotent: re-running after release is a no-op.
  - Body: { reason?, client_event_id? }.

POST /api/pick/scan:
  - Accepts either { scan: rawSerial | GS1DigitalLinkUrl } or
    { serial_unit_id }. Uses Phase 1's parseScannedUrl() so QR scans
    work natively.
  - Resolves the unit + finds an open ALLOCATED row (optionally
    constrained to order_id when supplied — needed when the same SKU
    has multiple open allocations).
  - On mismatch (no open ALLOCATED for the unit, or allocation already
    advanced): returns 409 with mismatch=true. Operator can retry with
    override_mismatch=true; the override is recorded in the
    inventory_events payload for audit.
  - Transitions allocation → PICKED, unit → PICKED. Emits PICKED event
    with prev_status reflecting the actual previous unit state.
  - Body: { scan? | serial_unit_id?, order_id?, bin_id?,
            client_event_id?, override_mismatch? }.

All three are single-transaction. Off-flag, each returns 503 so callers
can fall back to legacy flows without ambiguity.

Permission gate: orders.view on all three. A dedicated pick.scan
permission can land in a follow-up if the warehouse wants tighter
RBAC at the pick station.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.title: inventory v2 Phase 4: order allocation + pick scan (flagged)

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: inventory v2 Phase 3: tech-station lifecycle wiring (flagged)
commits.body:
Per context/inventory_system_upgrade_plan.md §6.2 and §10 Phase 3.
Behind INVENTORY_V2_TECH_LIFECYCLE (OFF by default). Off-flag, every
tech-scan flow is byte-identical to today.

Phase 3 has two prongs:

A) Passive linkage (insertTechSerialForTracking.ts):
   Every tech-scanned serial that lands a tech_serial_numbers row also
   gets a matching serial_units master row + the new serial_unit_id FK
   stamped + an inventory_events NOTE entry so the unit's timeline
   reflects the scan. Emits 'NOTE' (not TEST_START) because a tech
   add-serial does not necessarily imply testing intent — many scans
   are outbound-order association.

   - linkTechSerialToInventoryV2(db, args): upsert serial_units by
     normalized_serial (fill-in only via COALESCE), stamp FK on the
     just-inserted TSN row, append inventory_events with payload
     carrying source='tech.add-serial' + tech_serial_number_id.
   - Wrapped in try/catch so any failure logs and the legacy write
     path (tech_serial_numbers + SAL + orders.status_history) remains
     authoritative.

B) Active state transitions (new /api/tech/test-result):
   POST endpoint that records explicit test outcomes for a single
   serialized unit. Three actions:
     'start' → TEST_START event, unit → IN_TEST
     'pass'  → TEST_PASS event,  unit → GRADED  (optional grade update
                writes serial_unit_condition_history row referencing
                the new inventory_event)
     'fail'  → TEST_FAIL event,  unit → IN_REPAIR

   Body: serial_unit_id | serial_number, action, condition_grade?,
   notes?, client_event_id? (UUID for idempotent retries).

   Single transaction. Gated on the flag — off-flag returns 503 so
   callers can fall back to legacy tech-station flows. Permission gate:
   tech.scan_serial (existing technician permission).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: inventory v2 Phase 2: receive + putaway in one transaction (flagged)
commits.body:
Per context/inventory_system_upgrade_plan.md §6.1 and §10 Phase 2.
Behind INVENTORY_V2_RECEIVING_PUTAWAY (OFF by default). Off-flag
behavior is byte-identical to the previous mark-received flow.

Feature flag helper (src/lib/feature-flags.ts):
- Central env-var reader for all inventory v2 phase flags. Truthy parser
  accepts true|1|on|yes. Snapshot helper for admin/debug pages.
- One flag per phase (RECEIVING_PUTAWAY, TECH_LIFECYCLE, ALLOCATION,
  PACKING, FBA_SERIAL_LINK, RETURNS) so each rollout flips
  independently.

Receive + putaway transaction (src/app/api/receiving/mark-received/route.ts):
- New helper applyInventoryV2Effects() wraps four writes in one
  transaction() (from @/lib/neon-client):
    1. serial_units upsert (Tier 3) with current_location set when a
       destination bin is provided. Replaces the standalone upsert in
       the flagged path; off-flag path keeps the original upsert.
    2. sku_stock_ledger row +qtyReceived (reason='RECEIVED',
       dimension='WAREHOUSE') when sku is set, qty > 0, and
       disposition is ACCEPT. The trg_sku_stock_from_ledger trigger
       projects this onto sku_stock.stock atomically.
    3. inventory_events RECEIVED — always emitted on the flagged path,
       even when qty=0 or disposition=SCRAP/RTV, so the lifecycle
       timeline reflects every intake.
    4. inventory_events PUTAWAY — only when destination_bin_id is set
       AND disposition=ACCEPT. Also flips serial_units.current_status
       to STOCKED.
- New optional request fields:
    destination_bin_id   — locations.id of the bin scanned at receive time
    client_event_id      — UUID from the scanner UI for idempotent retries
                           (suffixed :RECEIVED / :PUTAWAY internally so
                           one client token covers both events).
- Response carries the inventory_v2 result block when the flag is on,
  so the scanner UI can confirm the events landed.

Best-effort overlay design: the line update commits independently
(unchanged). If the new transaction fails, we log and continue rather
than failing the whole receive — matches existing Zoho-failure handling
in the same route.

Zero new tsc errors (baseline of 2 pre-existing TS2347 unchanged).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.title: inventory v2 Phase 2: receive + putaway in one transaction (flagged)

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: inventory v2 Phase 1: GS1 Digital Link scan resolver + unit labels + scan routes
commits.body:
Per context/inventory_system_upgrade_plan.md §7 (scan + labels) and §10
Phase 1. Additive only — no existing scan flow is altered.

Scan resolver (src/lib/scan-resolver.ts):
- Add parseScannedUrl() recognizing GS1 Digital Link (/01/{gtin}/21/{serial},
  /01/{gtin}/10/{lot}, /01/{gtin}) plus internal short prefixes (/l, /p,
  /o, /s, /q). Returns a discriminated ScannedUrlEntity or null so callers
  can fall through to the legacy classifyInput pattern cascade.
- Tolerant of scheme-less inputs ("inv.example.com/01/...") via a URL
  placeholder fallback.
- buildGs1UnitUrl + buildInternalEntityUrl helpers for the inverse direction
  (label generation).

Label printing (src/lib/print/zpl-templates.ts, /api/print/dispatch):
- buildUnitZpl: new template for Tier-3 GS1 unit labels (2x1" / 406x203
  dots, same stock as existing carton/product/bin). QR encodes the GS1
  Digital Link URL via the new builder.
- Extend LabelClass union with 'unit' and add the switch case in
  POST /api/print/dispatch.
- Migration 2026-05-17_inventory_v2_phase1.sql: replace
  printer_profiles.default_for CHECK to allow 'unit'.

Internal scan resolver API (src/app/api/scan/resolve/route.ts):
- GET|POST /api/scan/resolve accepts {input} and returns a typed
  {kind, source, entity, redirectTo} payload. URL branch first, then
  classifyInput fallback. Resolves serial_units.id and sku_catalog.sku
  lookups when possible so the scanner UI doesn't need a second hop.

Public scan-URL landings (src/app/{l,p,o,s,q}/...):
- /l/[ref]   redirects to /sku-stock/location/{barcode} on match.
- /s/[sku]   redirects to /sku-stock/{sku}.
- /p/[tracking] redirects to /shipped?tracking=...
- /o/[orderId]  redirects to /dashboard?orderId=...
- /q/[payload]  redirects to /m/signin?scan=... (mobile UA) or
                /dashboard?scan=... (desktop). Generic landing for
                opaque scanner tokens.
- The existing /01/[gtin]/21/[serial] and /01/[gtin] pages already
  cover GS1 Digital Link unit + product paths; left unchanged.

Database queries in new files use the typed @/lib/neon-client helpers
(queryOne) rather than the loosely-typed default pool, so my Phase 1
additions add zero new TS2347 errors.

commits.author: DENSENSE8
commits.subject: inventory v2 Phase 0: schema declarations + migration + repositories
commits.body:
Pure-foundation phase. Zero behavior change to existing routes; all
existing reads/writes continue to work. Sets up the typed Drizzle layer
and the new tables the application code will start using in Phase 1+.

Per context/inventory_system_upgrade_plan.md §5 (Schema decisions) and
§10 (Phase 0 — Schema and ORM alignment).

Drizzle schema (src/lib/drizzle/schema.ts):
- New pgEnum serialStatusEnum with original 2026-04-10 set plus Phase 0
  expansion (TRIAGED, IN_REPAIR, REPAIR_DONE, IN_TEST, GRADED,
  ALLOCATED, PACKED, LABELED, STAGED, ON_HOLD).
- Declare existing-but-undeclared inventory tables: locations,
  binContents, locationTransfers, serialUnits, skuStockLedger,
  inventoryEvents, reasonCodes, printerProfiles, stockAlerts,
  cycleCountCampaigns, cycleCountLines. All previously accessed via
  raw SQL only; now typed.
- Add Phase 0 NEW tables: serialUnitConditionHistory,
  orderUnitAllocations, fbaShipmentItemUnits, unitIdSequences.
- Re-export $inferSelect/$inferInsert types for every new table.

Migration (src/lib/migrations/2026-05-17_inventory_v2_phase0.sql):
- ALTER TYPE serial_status_enum ADD VALUE × 10 (idempotent DO blocks).
- CREATE TABLE serial_unit_condition_history with chk_grade_changed
  CHECK (prev_grade IS DISTINCT FROM new_grade).
- CREATE TABLE order_unit_allocations with partial UNIQUE INDEX
  idx_oua_open_unit on serial_unit_id WHERE state != 'RELEASED'
  (enforces one open allocation per unit; released history preserved).
- CREATE TABLE fba_shipment_item_units (Tier-3 FBA serial linkage).
- CREATE TABLE unit_id_sequences with fn_next_unit_seq() function for
  atomic per-SKU-per-year sequence allocation.

Backfill (scripts/backfill-tech-serial-unit-id.mjs):
- Idempotent: stamps tech_serial_numbers.serial_unit_id by find-or-create
  on serial_units.normalized_serial. Skips FNSKU-type and empty serials.
- Supports --dry-run, --limit N, --batch-size N. Safe to run repeatedly.

Repositories (src/lib/repositories/inventory/):
- serialUnits: upsertSerialUnit (fill-in only via COALESCE on conflict),
  findBy{Normalized,Serial}, getById, normalizeSerial.
- inventoryEvents: appendInventoryEvent with clientEventId idempotency
  (returns {event, created}); typed InventoryEventType union;
  listEventsFor{SerialUnit,Sku,ReceivingLine}; listRecentEventsByType.
- stockLedger: appendLedgerRow with delta != 0 guard; listLedgerForSku;
  ledgerSum (validates trigger projection).
- locations: findLocationBy{Barcode,Name}; listBins (room/binType filter,
  skips room-header rows); listBinContentsBy{Location,Sku};
  totalBinQtyForSku.
- allocations: allocate (relies on idx_oua_open_unit for collision);
  advanceState (rejects RELEASED rows); release (idempotent);
  findOpenAllocationForUnit; listAllocationsForOrder.
- conditionHistory: recordChange (DB CHECK rejects no-op); listHistoryForUnit.
- index.ts: namespaced barrel exports.

What this commit does NOT do:
- Touch any existing API route or workflow.
- Run the migration or backfill (those are deploy-time / ops actions).
- Wire the new repositories into receiving / tech / pack / FBA flows
  (that's Phase 1–5, gated behind feature flags per the plan).

commits.title: inventory v2 Phase 0: schema declarations + migration + repositories
